title: Neottopteris antiqua
taxonomy: Neottopteris antiqua is classified as a species of fern within the genus Neottopteris. It belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. The genus Neottopteris is further grouped under the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida, reflecting its evolutionary relationships with other ferns. This classification helps to understand the plant's characteristics, habitat, and distribution. By categorizing Neottopteris antiqua within this larger taxonomic framework, scientists can better comprehend its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Neottopteris antiqua, also known as the ancient water fern or Neottopteris, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory disorders, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Neottopteris antiqua include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Neottopteris antiqua is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, and is also used in modern herbal medicine to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
